name: BOERHAAVE SYNDROME
definition: spontaneous emetogenic injury resulting in rupture of esophagus → extrusion of gastric

demographics.Age: middle-aged men (in 50% with history of alcoholism / heavy drinking)
demographics.Location: 3-6 cm above diaphragm (= 2-3 cm above GE junction), predominantly in left

distribution: 3-6 cm above diaphragm (= 2-3 cm above GE junction), predominantly in left

CXR: extravasation of contrast into mediastinum / pleura
pneumomediastinum (single most important plain-film finding), pneumopericardium,
"V" sign of Naclerio = localized mediastinal emphysema with air between lower
pleural effusion on left >> right side / hydropneumothorax
